meaning: n.潦草的写法,潦草写成的东西,杂文v.潦草地写,乱写,滥写
examples: How do you expect me to read this scribble?
I'm sorry what I wrote was such a scribble.
Classic copyright protection laws provide that even if you scribble on paper, you will automatically own the copyright of the information on that paper.
The mysterious scribble, dated 23 January 1898, was uncovered as the first panels were taken down.
Scribble them out on post-its and stick them on gator boards all around the room.
